CHIME per-burst ACF de-scalloping fix + RFI investigation + interactive window editor build (msgs e1–e133)

## De-scalloping the per-burst figures (e1–e48)

Continuing from the prior chunk's finding that the coarse-channel comb (0.39 MHz sinusoids) persisted because the diagnostic configs had de-scalloping OFF. The agent read `normalize_bandpass` in `freya_scintillation.py`: it divides each fine channel by its off-pulse mean — the per-channel off-pulse mean IS the static instrumental bandpass (system gain incl. PFB scallop), so dividing cancels the scallop exactly while leaving time-variable scintillation intact. Guard: needs ≥50 off-pulse bins else raises ValueError; masks non-positive/gain-starved channels.

Config-flag audit across 14 `_chime*.yaml` (bandpass_norm.enable | grid_reg.enable): casey F|F, casey_hi F|F, chromatica F|T, freya F|T, **freya_hi T|T (only one with bandpass on)**, hamilton F|T, isha F|T, johndoeII F|T, mahi F|T, oran F|T, phineas F|T, whitney F|T, wilhelm F|T, zach F|T. Root cause confirmed: agent loaded diagnostic configs with de-scalloping off.

**Fix to `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/perburst_acf_figures.py`:** renamed config builder `force_4_equal_snr_subbands` → `force_provenance_complete` (forces num_subbands=4, use_snr_subbanding=True, grid_regularization.enable=True, bandpass_normalization.enable=True). `make_figure` builds the de-scalloped spectrum with a ValueError fallback: when off-pulse <50 bins (wide bursts), apply a per-channel **time-median flat-field** EXCLUDING on-pulse columns (`colmask[burst_lims[0]:burst_lims[1]]=False`) so a wide burst doesn't bias the gain estimate. Title/metadata record `descallop_method`. ACF-stage ref fixed (cfg already provenance-complete). MAXTICKS fix via `ax_dyn.yaxis.set_major_locator(plt.MaxNLocator(8))`. Burst list parameterized via `ONLY_BURSTS` env var.

**First regen** (background exec `d6432d0e-da05-44ae-a617-b1a8abad3608`): all 12 OK; 8 de-scalloped via pipeline off-pulse mean (casey, whitney, phineas, freya, zach, chromatica, wilhelm, hamilton), 4 wide bursts (mahi off [0,18], oran [60,95], johndoeII [10,30], isha [10,45]) fell back to NON-de-scalloped (fallback not yet implemented) + MAXTICKS warnings. freya verified comb-gone. isha still showed full comb, labeled "NOT de-scalloped".

Then implemented time-median fallback (so `descalloped` always True; `descallop_method` = "off-pulse mean (pipeline bandpass_normalization)" or "time-median flat-field (off-pulse < 50 bins)"). **Second regen** (exec `03e3865c-8686-444b-a701-31d43715838b`): all 12 de-scalloped, four via fallback. Verified isha (e41): comb removed, on-pulse spike ~bin 55, subband centers 441/533/631/739 MHz (~79–80 MHz bw), edge tick-bars fixed.

Copied 12 PNGs + `perburst_acf_figures.py` + `perburst_meta.json` to workspace `perburst_figs/`.

## RFI investigation — user's hunch that RFI dominates the ACF (e49–e91)

USER (e49): "One obvious thing jumps out and it's that each of the dynamic spectra you're showing are still cluttered with RFI --- wouldn't shock me if that was completely dominating the ACF"

Agent tested directly rather than assuming. For freya: 15.7% channels already masked; after de-scalloping the 10 residual bright (|z|>8) on-pulse channels contribute only **0.2%** of on-pulse deviation power — a persistent bright channel normalizes to ≈1 under the flat-field, so it's no longer an ACF outlier; imshow stretch makes stripes look dominant but the ACF barely sees them.

**RFI power-share across all 12** (fraction of on-pulse ACF deviation power from |z|>8 channels): casey 0.0%, whitney 1.0%, phineas 3.5%, mahi 0.5%, freya 0.2%, zach 0.8%, chromatica 0.0%, wilhelm 1.0%, **oran 21.6% (1448 bright channels)**, hamilton 1.1%, johndoeII 1.3%, isha 0.4%. So 11/12 ≤3.5%; only oran high.

Pipeline `mask_rfi` (core.py:260): frequency-domain channel flagging from off-burst statistics at `freq_threshold_sigma=5.0`, iterative (5 passes), `rfi_downsample_factor` default 8 (16 in configs), with `enable_time_domain_flagging=false` in all configs. Aggressive test (freq_threshold_sigma 5→3.5 + time-domain flagging ON): barely moved anything — 11/12 stayed ≤1.5%, oran stayed 21%. Conclusion: oran's contaminant is time-variable RFI inside the on-pulse window that frequency-domain flagging (from off-burst stats) can't catch.

**oran characterization (decisive, e63–e64):** of 1448 bright on-pulse channels, only 242 (3% of ACF power) are persistent (also bright off-pulse = real RFI, safe to mask); the other 1206 (18.6%) are bright ONLY during the 74 ms burst = intrinsic burst spectral structure. Masking them would destroy real signal. So even oran's contaminant is NOT RFI.

**Net ruling: RFI does not dominate the ACF for any burst.** Recorded in memory (`mem_1d4ba27aaf20`, `mem_d88ce6e5376d`).

Added to figures: RFI-power-share annotation (green if ≤10%, red if >10%) via `ax_dyn.text(...)`, tightened display clip from 99th→97.5th percentile, recorded `rfi_acf_power_share` in metadata. Regenerated all 12 (exec `a37d5650-b222-4dfb-b967-4ba1991c6991`). Confirmed annotations: casey 0.0%, whitney 0.97%, phineas 3.48%, mahi 0.47%, freya 0.2%, zach 0.81%, chromatica 0.02%, wilhelm 1.0%, oran 21.65% (red), hamilton 1.09%, johndoeII 1.34%, isha 0.38%.

**Saved 14 artifacts** (e90): 12 PNGs + driver + meta. Full IDs in Literals below.

## User pivots to manual window/RFI guidance (e92–e133)

USER (e95): "There's still lots of RFI visible in the dynamic spectra"
USER (e97): "Furthermore, the on-pulse regions are sometimes way too wide, sometimes way too narrow"
USER (e101): "It might be helpful if I could guide the borders manually via some interactive tool"

Agent searched skills — no interactive-plot/region-selection app in catalog (only ketcher-chemistry sketcher connected). Built a purpose-made three-piece browser workflow:

1. **`make_window_tool_assets.py`** — renders one de-scalloped dynamic-spectrum PNG (magma, origin-lower, display-downsampled to ~900×500) + `<name>_disp.json` (profile, ntime, nchan, fmin, fmax, burst_lims, off_lims, descallop_method, vmin, vmax) per burst, ONE burst/process to bound RAM. Ran all 12 (exec `fed54a70-138d-4d04-aed8-d47eb5d78a48`). Per-burst dims recorded in Literals — notably casey ntime=1758 nchan=16320 burst=(889,898) off=(0,860); oran ntime=337 nchan=130816 burst=(100,250) off=(60,95); mahi nchan=522240; johndoeII nchan=521728; isha nchan=261888. Confirms default-window problems: oran on-pulse 150 bins (too wide), casey 9 bins with 860-bin off-pulse.

2. **`build_window_tool.py`** — embeds all 12 PNGs (base64) + metadata into one self-contained `window_editor.html` (10.6 MB). Browser UI: dropdown burst select; modes "on-pulse border"/"off-pulse border" (drag blue/grey time-window borders), "paint RFI band" (drag vertically to mask frequency band, red); "clear RFI", "reset windows", "download choices JSON" (→ `window_choices.json` = `{burst:{burst_lims,off_lims,rfi_bands_mhz}}`). Coordinate mapping: fractional x→bin=round(frac*ntime), fractional y→freq=fmin+frac*(fmax-fmin), windows stored in full time bins.

3. **`perburst_acf_manual.py`** — consumes `window_choices.json`: rebuilds de-scalloped spectrum forcing user windows via `manual_burst_window`/`manual_noise_window`, uses time-median fallback when chosen off-window <50 bins, masks channels whose center freq falls in any painted RFI band (ON TOP of existing pipeline mask: `existing_mask | painted_bands`), recomputes 4 equal-S/N subband ACFs, re-renders same 3-panel figure with RFI diagnostic. Outputs `<name>_acf_windows_manual.png` + `perburst_meta_manual.json`.

Smoke test with synthetic choices (freya burst [251,270] off [10,200] RFI [[625,632]]; casey burst [886,900] off [400,860]): first freya render had EMPTY ACF panel — agent had guessed wrong dict keys. Read the working auto driver (e125): correct keys are `subband_lags_mhz`, `subband_acfs`, `subband_num_channels`, `subband_channel_widths_mhz`, `subband_channel_slices` (NO `lags_mhz`, NO `subband_bandwidths_mhz`; subband bw = `subband_num_channels[i]*subband_channel_widths_mhz[i]`). Fixed ACF-plotting block to match auto driver exactly (COMB=0.390625, LAG_MAX=0.5, high-freq-at-top ordering, per-panel lag-0 normalization, comb harmonic guides, step=1.25). Re-tested freya (e131/e133): all correct — manual windows applied, RFI band masked at ~627 MHz (red stripe + channel excluded from ACF), de-scalloping applied, all 4 subband ACFs render with narrow lag-0 spike into flat floor. Full loop validated.

Saved 4 artifacts (window_editor.html, make_window_tool_assets.py, build_window_tool.py, perburst_acf_manual.py — IDs in Literals) and copied editor to `~/Desktop/window_editor.html`. Recorded workflow in memory (`mem_59c257db2164`, `mem_378123ae2a04`).

- Agent noted (not yet acted): RFI bands are painted in frequency (whole-channel across all time); offered to add a time-localized RFI paint mode if needed — awaiting user.
- zach's marginal sub-3σ excess (sep_z=2.67) flagged for bandpass-normalized re-check (carried from prior chunk).

Reading this honestly: **the RFI cleaning is working as intended** (off-pulse ACF collapses toward zero everywhere it's measurable; zero burst-signal overlap in all 12) — but **the joint Lorentzian fits are not reliable**. Look at the pattern:

- **α pinned at a fit boundary (1.0 or 8.0) in 8 of 12 bursts** — that's not a measurement, it's the optimizer hitting its bounds because the data doesn't constrain the shape.
- **γ₀ pinned at its floor (0.01 MHz) in 4 bursts** (casey, freya, zach, wilhelm) — collapsed to unresolved.
- **Reduced χ² is 3–29 in most cases** — poor fits even where the parameters aren't railed.
- Only **oran** (α=4.45±1.39, redχ²=24, still bad χ²) and **phineas** (redχ²=1.32, but α pinned at 8) come close to an interior, credible optimum, and neither is clean either.

This matches the multi-burst instrumental-null result from earlier in this project: for 9 of 10 fittable bursts, the on-pulse ACF reproduces the off-pulse noise floor. Cleaning the RFI harder doesn't create a resolved scintillation signal that isn't there — it just makes the noise floor cleaner, which is exactly what we're seeing (off-pulse ACF → white) while the joint fit still fails to land on an interior optimum for most bursts.

--- msg[20] assistant ---
This confirms it visually: phineas' top subband spikes to >2 at lag≈0 then collapses to a flat, noisy floor within ~0.3 MHz — a narrow, high-amplitude spike consistent with residual self-noise/channel decorrelation, not a resolved scintillation decay. The fit tracks that spike (γ=0.04 MHz) but α pins at the upper bound (8.0) because there's no real frequency-scaling information across subbands to constrain it — three of the four subbands are essentially flat noise.

**Bottom line: even with the properly-validated automatic RFI cleaning, no burst produces a credible joint fit.** 8 of 12 have α pinned at a fit boundary; 4 of 12 have γ₀ pinned at its floor. This isn't a failure of the cleaning — the off-pulse ACF null and burst-overlap checks both pass everywhere — it's confirmation that **there is no resolved scintillation signal to fit**, consistent with the earlier multi-burst instrumental-null result (9/10 fittable bursts reproduce their off-pulse noise floor).

Two confounds worth flagging before drawing conclusions from the numbers themselves:

1. **11 of 12 bursts still carry default (unedited) on/off-pulse windows** — only casey's were validated through the tool. That's likely why the RFI counts swing so widely (125 for casey vs. 6781 for johndoeII): a badly-placed off-pulse window feeds bad statistics into the flagger.
1. **γ₀'s fit floor (0.01 MHz) is an arbitrary lmfit bound**, not a physical value — bursts pinned there should be read as "unresolved, γ₀ < 0.01 MHz" (an upper limit), not as a measurement of 0.01 MHz.

Looking across all 12, two patterns stand out:

**Most bursts (casey, freya, zach, wilhelm, chromatica, hamilton, phineas, isha, mahi, johndoeII) show a narrow high-amplitude spike at lag≈0 collapsing within ~0.1–0.3 MHz into a flat, noise-like floor** — sometimes elevated (casey's ~0.2–0.25 plateau, zach's ~0.15–0.25) but not *decaying* with lag, which is the intrinsic-envelope signature from before, not scintillation. The joint fit tries to fit a single γ(ν) shape across four subbands that mostly look like this, which is exactly why α keeps pinning at 1 or 8 and χ² is bad — there's no consistent frequency-dependent shape for it to lock onto.

**Two bursts look qualitatively different and deserve a closer look:**
- **oran** (742 MHz subband): a smooth, genuinely decaying correlation from ~150 down to ~10–20 over the full 5 MHz window, with real curvature — not a flat plateau. Its fit lands at **α = 4.45 ± 1.39**, close to Kolmogorov (4.4) or thin-screen (4.0) expectations, though χ²ᵥ=24 is still poor (likely because the lower three subbands are flat/noisy and don't share that shape).
- **whitney** (615 MHz subband): a smooth downward trend over the full lag range that the fit doesn't track at all — could be a real broad scintle or a residual slow drift; needs isolated diagnosis.

Both of these are still on **default windows**, so before reading physics into either one, they need real on/off-pulse windows validated the way casey's were.
